summary: vue-demi-fix exfiltrates installer identity to hardcoded bare-IP endpoint via preinstall/postinstall
details: package.json declares preinstall and postinstall lifecycle hooks that invoke wget against a hardcoded plain-HTTP endpoint at the bare IP 109.71.252.153:8080, transmitting the installer's username ($(whoami)), current working directory ($(pwd)), hostname ($(hostname)), and package name as URL query parameters. This fires automatically on `npm install` with no user interaction. The package itself ships no functionality — index.js only prints a disclosure-style message — and the name impersonates the widely-used `vue-demi` package, indicating typosquat delivery of an install-time reconnaissance beacon to attacker-controlled infrastructure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
